description: Best-effort proposed observations and characteristics grouped by first character of data/hash-types filename. These are review proposals only and are not loaded by dahash runtime.

entries.name: LastPass CLI

extractor_candidates.note: LastPass hashes depend on local storage/export source and iteration/email fields.
LastPass hashes depend on local storage/export source and iteration/email fields.

accuracy_notes: Add parser validation for separator count and per-field length/alphabet instead of relying only on whole-line regex.
Promote unique prefix or wrapper match above generic length/alphabet matches.
Use John format label as a tool hint but not as sole runtime identification evidence.
Use native artifact/extractor context as an observation signal when the input comes from a file, capture, dump, or database source.

entries.name: LastPass / LastPass sniffed

extractor_candidates.note: LastPass hashes depend on local storage/export source and iteration/email fields.

official_notes: Module usage notice: `The hash used here is not the one sent via e.g. the web interface to LastPass servers (pbkdf2_sha256_hex (pbkdf2_sha256 ($pass, $email, $iterations), $pass, 1) but instead the one stored (by e.g. your browser or the pocket version) to disk. For instance, Opera and Chrome store the hash in local SQLite databases; Firefox uses files ending with 'lpall.slps' - for Linux: 2nd line is interesting / base64 decode it; for Windows, see https://hashcat.net/forum/thread-2701-post-16111.html#pid16111 - and_key.itr`

extractor_candidates.note: LastPass hashes depend on local storage/export source and iteration/email fields.

accuracy_notes: Add parser validation for separator count and per-field length/alphabet instead of relying only on whole-line regex.
Cross-check Hashcat mode example shape before raising confidence for ambiguous raw hashes.
Use native artifact/extractor context as an observation signal when the input comes from a file, capture, dump, or database source.
Review official Hashcat mode usage/extraction notes before importing this observation.

entries.name: LDAP (SSHA-256)

extractor_candidates.note: Private-key formats are extractor output; PEM/OpenSSH header context matters.

special_native_output_notes.note: LDAP-style hashes often include a braced scheme prefix such as {SHA}, {SSHA}, {MD5}, {SMD5}, {CRYPT}; that prefix should outrank generic base64/hex length observations.

extractor_candidates.note: Private-key formats are extractor output; PEM/OpenSSH header context matters.

entries.name: LM

special_native_output_notes.note: Windows dump lines may include DOMAIN\user:RID:LM:NTLM:::; raw 32-hex alone is ambiguous. Presence of RID, LM slot aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee, domain separator, or $NT$ prefix should raise NTLM confidence.

native_notes.note: Windows dump lines may include DOMAIN\user:RID:LM:NTLM:::; raw 32-hex alone is ambiguous. Presence of RID, LM slot aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee, domain separator, or $NT$ prefix should raise NTLM confidence.

accuracy_notes: Add parser validation for separator count and per-field length/alphabet instead of relying only on whole-line regex.

Raw hex-only forms need context-aware ranking because many algorithms share the same length and alphabet.

Review official Hashcat mode usage/extraction notes before importing this observation.

entries.name: LUKS v1 (legacy)

extractor_candidates.note: LUKS formats must preserve header/keyslot metadata; raw hex should be low confidence.

official_notes: Module usage notice: `The file https://hashcat.net/misc/example_hashes/hashcat_luks_testfiles.7z actually contains several examples of the different hash+cipher combinations. The password is stored in the pw file. That file is automatically used by tools/test.sh`
